382	
383	== Core Principles
384	
385	The Axial Lexicon provides reusable, atomic terminology for technical specifications.
386	Each term maintains precise semantics while adapting to implementation context.
387	Specifications selectively import only needed axial terms, avoiding vocabulary bloat.
388	
389	== Motif Vocabulary
390	
391	AXL terms are
392	{axl_motif_s}
393	- abstract patterns that concrete specifications voice in their local context.
394	
395	[[axl_motif]]
396	{axl_motif}::
397	An abstract pattern defined in the Axial Lexicon that can be voiced by concrete terms in specifications.
398	A
399	{axl_motif}
400	captures the essential shape of a concept while remaining independent of implementation context.
401	Specifications voice
402	{axl_motif_s}
403	through local terms that bind the abstract pattern to specific environments.
404	
405	[[axl_voicing]]
406	{axl_voicing}::
407	A concrete term in a specification that gives local expression to A
408	{axl_motif}.
409	Each
410	{axl_voicing}
411	adapts the abstract pattern to a specific context while preserving the
412	{axl_motif_s}
413	essential character.
414	
415	[[axl_voices]]
416	{axl_voices}::
417	The relationship where a concrete term expresses a
418	{axl_motif}
419	in a specific context.
420	When a term
421	{axl_voices}
422	a
423	{axl_motif},
424	it gives the abstract pattern local form and implementation-specific behavior.
425	
426	[[axl_evokes]]
427	{axl_evokes}::
428	The relationship where A
429	{axl_motif}
430	calls forth its
431	{axl_voicing_s}
432	in the reader's understanding.
433	When encountering A
434	{axl_motif},
435	it
436	{axl_evokes}
437	the concrete realizations known from various specifications.
438	
439	[[axl_annotation]]
440	{axl_annotation}::
441	A
442	{xref_MCM}
443	comment line that declares the
444	{axl_motif_s}
445	a term
446	{axl_voices}.
447	The
448	{axl_annotation}
449	appears between anchor and definition as a space-separated list of prefix terms on a comment line with no space after `//`.
450	+
451	Format: `//relationship primary [dimension ...]`
452	+
453	* First term: the relationship (typically `axl_voices`)
454	* Second term: the primary
455	{axl_motif}
456	being voiced
457	* Remaining terms: dimension terms (prefixed `axd_`) specifying characteristics
458	+
459	Examples:
460	+
461	* `//axl_voices axc_call` - simple voicing
462	* `//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable` - voicing with dimensions
463	+
464	Category prefixes carry semantic meaning, eliminating need for punctuation.
465	Position determines role: relationship first, primary second, dimensions following.
466	This design prioritizes LLM interpretability over human visual parsing.
467	When ambiguity or friction arises during LLM processing, the format should evolve to resolve it.
468	
469	== Motif Implications
470	
471	{axl_motif_s}
472	carry implied characteristics beyond their definitions.
473	These implications guide how
474	{axl_voicing_s}
475	should behave:
476	
477	* Anchor placement expectations per
478	{xref_MCM}
479	form patterns
480	* Type/instance level - where the
481	{axl_motif}
482	sits in abstraction hierarchy
483	* Contextual requirements - parent structures or operations the
484	{axl_motif}
485	exists within
486	* Voicing expectation - whether the
487	{axl_motif}
488	typically requires local voicing
489	
490	== Form Expectations
491	
492	Each AXL category implies an expected
493	{xref_MCM}
494	definition form pattern for documents using that category.
495	During type-scheme linting, these expectations guide validation of definition structure.
496	
497	* {axc_*} control terms expect
498	{xref_MCM}
499	`mcm_form_deflist`
500	* {axj_structure} definitions expect
501	{xref_MCM}
502	`mcm_form_section`
503	* {axj_field} definitions expect
504	{xref_MCM}
505	`mcm_form_bullet` under parent structure
506	* {axk_premise} definitions expect
507	{xref_MCM}
508	`mcm_form_deflist`
509	* Most other categories expect
510	{xref_MCM}
511	`mcm_form_deflist`
512	
513	These expectations support the
514	{axl_annotation}
515	pattern where documents include `//axl_voices motif ...` markers that are validated when AXL is in context.
516	
517	== Axial Control Terms
518	
519	[[axc_variable]]
520	{axc_variable}::
521	A
522	{axl_motif}
523	representing a temporary value holder denoted by distinctive delimiters that exists for the duration of an operational sequence.
524	Implementation varies by context: shell variables in bash, dictionary entries in Python, sessionStorage in browser JavaScript.
525	
526	[[axc_fatal]]
527	{axc_fatal}::
528	A
529	{axl_motif}
530	representing immediate termination of the operational sequence with error status, preventing all subsequent steps from executing.
531	Requires operator intervention or error recovery before retry.
532	
533	[[axc_warn]]
534	{axc_warn}::
535	A
536	{axl_motif}
537	representing display of a non-blocking warning message while allowing the operational sequence to continue execution.
538	Alerts operators to concerning conditions without halting progress.
539	
540	[[axc_store]]
541	{axc_store}::
542	A
543	{axl_motif}
544	representing capture of a value from an operation result or computation into a
545	{axc_variable}
546	for later reference.
547	Context determines storage mechanism while preserving semantic intent.
548	
549	[[axc_use]]
550	{axc_use}::
551	A
552	{axl_motif}
553	representing reference to a previously stored
554	{axc_variable}
555	value in the current operation.
556	Retrieves stored values from context-appropriate storage location.
557	
558	[[axc_require]]
559	{axc_require}::
560	A
561	{axl_motif}
562	representing validation check that confirms an expected condition, triggering
563	{axc_fatal}
564	or alternate execution path if not met.
565	Enforces preconditions and invariants throughout execution.
566	
567	[[axc_poll]]
568	{axc_poll}::
569	A
570	{axl_motif}
571	representing repeated execution of a status check at defined intervals until completion condition or timeout.
572	Implements retry logic with configurable intervals and limits.
573	
574	[[axc_call]]
575	{axc_call}::
576	A
577	{axl_motif}
578	representing direct invocation of an external service, API endpoint, or command.
579	Captures both success and error responses for processing.
580	
581	[[axc_submit]]
582	{axc_submit}::
583	A
584	{axl_motif}
585	representing initiation of an asynchronous operation that returns immediately with a handle for later completion tracking.
586	Distinct from
587	{axc_call}
588	which expects synchronous response.
589	
590	[[axc_await]]
591	{axc_await}::
592	A
593	{axl_motif}
594	representing blocking wait on a submitted asynchronous operation until completion.
595	Polls operation status until done, then returns final result or error.
596	
597	[[axc_show]]
598	{axc_show}::
599	A
600	{axl_motif}
601	representing presentation of operational facts or values to the operator.
602	Implementation varies from console output to UI display based on context.
603	
604	[[axc_step]]
605	{axc_step}::
606	A
607	{axl_motif}
608	representing a normal progress message indicating successful completion of a processing stage.
609	Provides operational visibility without implying warnings or errors.
610	
611	== Axial Environment Terms
612	
613	{axe_environment_s}
614	specify execution contexts for control operations.
615	{axl_voicing_s}
616	of
617	{axc_call},
618	{axc_warn},
619	{axc_fatal}
620	and similar control motifs describe step-level behavior within operations.
621	
622	[[axe_rest_api]]
623	{axe_rest_api}::
624	A
625	{axl_motif}
626	representing invocation of an HTTP REST API endpoint.
627	Request/response via JSON or other structured format.
628	Authentication via bearer tokens, API keys, or similar.
629	Calls return HTTP status codes and response bodies.
630	
631	[[axe_console]]
632	{axe_console}::
633	A
634	{axl_motif}
635	representing direct terminal invocation.
636	Standard output and standard error are visible to the user.
637	Output can be routed directly to stdout/stderr.
638	
639	[[axe_mcp_transport]]
640	{axe_mcp_transport}::
641	A
642	{axl_motif}
643	representing invocation through the MCP (Model Context Protocol) transport layer.
644	Standard error is swallowed by the transport — all output must be captured in a return buffer to reach the caller.
645	
646	[[axe_daemon_runtime]]
647	{axe_daemon_runtime}::
648	A
649	{axl_motif}
650	representing a long-running system process.
651	Errors are logged, not displayed.
652	Fatal means graceful shutdown with cleanup.
653	No human in immediate feedback loop.
654	
655	== Axial Operation Terms
656	
657	[[axo_procedure]]
658	{axo_procedure}::
659	A
660	{axl_motif}
661	representing an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
662	Standalone operations not affiliated with an entity.
663	Voiced at the
664	{axl_definition_site}
665	via
666	{axvo_procedure}.
667	
668	[[axo_role]]
669	{axo_role}::
670	A
671	{axl_motif}
672	representing an operational identity pattern that defines capabilities and permissions.
673	Determines available operations and access boundaries.
674	
675	[[axo_identity]]
676	{axo_identity}::
677	A
678	{axl_motif}
679	representing the abstract concept of an operational identity.
680	An entity capable of authentication and holding authorities within a system.
681	{axo_identity}
682	is the pattern;
683	{axo_actor_s}
684	are named instantiations with specific bindings.
685	
686	[[axo_actor]]
687	{axo_actor}::
688	A
689	{axl_motif}
690	representing a specific named identity with defined responsibilities and exclusive authority over an operational domain.
691	{axo_actor_s}
692	instantiate
693	{axo_identity}
694	with concrete bindings (human via OAuth, service via service account) and permissions.
695	
696	[[axo_entity]]
697	{axo_entity}::
698	A
699	{axl_motif}
700	representing an object with both structure and behavior.
701	Unlike
702	{axr_record}
703	which captures pure data,
704	{axo_entity}
705	includes methods, builder patterns, or lifecycle operations.
706	{axl_voicing_s}
707	describe the entity's data members and its behavioral interface (construction, mutation, registration).
708	Typical manifestations include Rust structs with `impl` blocks, builder-pattern APIs, or objects that participate in registration protocols.
709	
710	== Axial Type Terms
711	
712	[[axt_type]]
713	{axt_type}::
714	A
715	{axl_motif}
716	representing a data format constraint that defines valid values and structures.
717	Enforces data validation and enables type-safe operations.
718	
719	[[axt_string]]
720	{axt_string}::
721	A
722	{axl_motif}
723	representing text data type with optional pattern validation.
724	{axl_voicing_s}
725	may address context-specific concerns including:
726	+
727	* Encoding (ASCII, UTF-8, locale-dependent character sets)
728	* Termination semantics (null-terminated, length-prefixed, delimiter-bounded)
729	* Mutability (immutable values, mutable buffers)
730	* Memory management (owned, borrowed, reference-counted)
731	* Safety constraints (shell-safe, URL-safe, JSON-safe, SQL-safe)
732	* Format patterns (timestamps, identifiers, hashes, paths)
733	* Size and bounds (fixed-length, maximum length, unbounded)
734	
735	[[axt_number]]
736	{axt_number}::
737	A
738	{axl_motif}
739	representing numeric value type supporting integers and floating point.
740	May specify range constraints or precision requirements.
741	
742	[[axt_boolean]]
743	{axt_boolean}::
744	A
745	{axl_motif}
746	representing binary true/false value type.
747	Maps to language-specific boolean implementations.
748	
749	[[axt_array]]
750	{axt_array}::
751	A
752	{axl_motif}
753	representing ordered collection of typed elements.
754	Specifies element type and optional size constraints.
755	
756	[[axt_object]]
757	{axt_object}::
758	A
759	{axl_motif}
760	representing named field collection forming a composite type.
761	Contains typed fields with defined cardinality.
762	
763	[[axt_map]]
764	{axt_map}::
765	A
766	{axl_motif}
767	representing a dictionary structure with typed keys and typed values.
768	2-arity: reads two
769	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
770	from following text — the first identifies the key type, the second identifies the value type.
771	Keys are unique within the map; values are accessed by key lookup.
772	Serialization format determines key ordering guarantees and key type constraints (e.g., JSON restricts keys to strings).
773	
774	[[axt_enumeration]]
775	{axt_enumeration}::
776	A
777	{axl_motif}
778	representing constrained set of valid string values.
779	Restricts values to predefined options.
780	
781	[[axt_enum_value]]
782	{axt_enum_value}::
783	A
784	{axl_motif}
785	representing single allowed option within an
786	{axt_enumeration}.
787	Must be explicitly listed in enumeration definition.
788	+
789	Encoding is carried by an
790	{axd_dimension}
791	on the voicing line — e.g.,
792	{axd_string}
793	for string-token-encoded values.
794	The encoding
795	{axd_dimension}
796	specifies the
797	{mcm_sprue}
798	arity for the wire-level value.
799	Exactly one encoding dimension must qualify every
800	{axt_enum_value}
801	voicing whose
802	{axt_enumeration}
803	participates in a wire form.
804	
805	[[axt_insignia]]
806	{axt_insignia}::
807	A
808	{axl_motif}
809	representing a glyph-prefixed typed identity string with seed-based allocation.
810	Extends
811	{axt_string}
812	with structural properties: a unicode verification glyph (decorative in input, mandatory in output), a fixed encoding format, a monotonic seed allocation mechanism, and optional hierarchical embedding (parent identity encoded as a prefix substring).
813	+
814	Every
815	{axt_insignia}
816	declares an encoding nature via
817	{axd_dimension_s}:
818	{axd_algebraic}
819	or
820	{axd_temporal}.
821	
822	[[axt_variant]]
823	{axt_variant}::
824	A
825	{axl_motif}
826	representing a value that can take one of several distinct forms, where each form may have different structure.
827	Unlike
828	{axt_enumeration}
829	which constrains to predefined strings,
830	{axt_variant}
831	allows structurally different alternatives.
832	{axl_voicing_s}
833	define the specific forms and discrimination logic for their context.
834	
835	== Axial Record Terms
836	
837	{axr_record_s}
838	and
839	{axr_member_s}
840	provide format-agnostic patterns for structured data.
841	Format-specific variants (JSON, protobuf, XML, etc.) capture serialization-specific semantics.
842	{axr_member_s}
843	inherit format context from their parent
844	{axr_record}.
845	
846	[[axr_record]]
847	{axr_record}::
848	A
849	{axl_motif}
850	representing a named collection of typed
851	{axr_member_s}.
852	Abstract pattern that format-specific variants voice with serialization semantics.
853	Use format-specific variants (
854	{axr_record_json},
855	{axr_record_protobuf},
856	etc.) when format is known.
857	
858	[[axr_record_json]]
859	{axr_record_json}::
860	A
861	{axl_motif}
862	representing a JSON object structure.
863	{axr_member_s}
864	are unordered, string-keyed properties.
865	Requires
866	{axd_nullable}
867	specification since JSON has explicit null.
868	
869	[[axr_record_protobuf]]
870	{axr_record_protobuf}::
871	A
872	{axl_motif}
873	representing a protobuf message.
874	{axr_member_s}
875	require
876	{axd_field_number}
877	and
878	{axd_wire_type}
879	dimensions.
880	Supports schema evolution through field numbering.
881	
882	[[axr_record_xml]]
883	{axr_record_xml}::
884	A
885	{axl_motif}
886	representing an XML element structure.
887	{axr_member_s}
888	require
889	{axd_xml_form}
890	to distinguish attributes from child elements.
891	Order may be significant depending on schema.
892	
893	[[axr_record_yaml]]
894	{axr_record_yaml}::
895	A
896	{axl_motif}
897	representing a YAML mapping.
898	Superset of JSON with anchors, aliases, and multi-document support.
899	{axr_member_s}
900	follow JSON-like semantics with extended syntax.
901	
902	[[axr_record_binary]]
903	{axr_record_binary}::
904	A
905	{axl_motif}
906	representing a binary/memory structure.
907	{axr_member_s}
908	require
909	{axd_endianness}
910	for multi-byte fields.
911	Fixed layout with explicit size and alignment.
912	
913	[[axr_member]]
914	{axr_member}::
915	A
916	{axl_motif}
917	representing a typed element within an
918	{axr_record}.
919	Inherits serialization format from parent
920	{axr_record}.
921	Default 1-arity: reads one
922	{mcm_sprue}
923	from following text — the serialized property name (e.g., the JSON key).
924	The
925	{mcm_sprue}
926	is the exact wire-level name; the
927	{mcm_quoin}
928	is the conceptual name.
929	Additional type motifs on the annotation line (e.g.,
930	{axt_map},
931	{axt_string})
932	contribute their own arity to the total read sequence:
933	{axr_member}
934	sprue first, then type motif lookaheads.
935	Qualified by
936	{axd_dimension_s}
937	in
938	{axl_annotation}:
939	`//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axt_string`
940	+
941	Under
942	{axr_record_json},
943	exactly one of
944	{axd_property}
945	or
946	{axd_positional}
947	must qualify the
948	{axr_member}.
949	{axd_property}
950	preserves the 1-arity sprue contract.
951	{axd_positional}
952	suppresses it (0-arity for sprue) since the member's value provides the parent map's key directly and no separate sprue exists in the wire form.
953	
954	== Axial JSON Terms
955	
956	[[axj_structure]]
957	{axj_structure}::
958	A
959	{axl_motif}
960	representing named schema definition for JSON objects.
961	Defines complete object shape with field specifications.
962	
963	[[axj_field]]
964	{axj_field}::
965	A
966	{axl_motif}
967	representing named, typed element within a
968	{axj_structure}.
969	Combines tag identifier with type constraint.
970	
971	[[axj_field_tag]]
972	{axj_field_tag}::
973	A
974	{axl_motif}
975	representing the key identifier for a
976	{axj_field}.
977	Follows naming conventions of target specification.
978	
979	[[axj_field_type]]
980	{axj_field_type}::
981	A
982	{axl_motif}
983	representing the value constraint for a
984	{axj_field}.
985	Determines parsing and validation behavior.
986	
987	[[axj_array_element]]
988	{axj_array_element}::
989	A
990	{axl_motif}
991	representing type constraint for members of an
992	{axt_array}
993	field.
994	Applies uniformly to all array elements.
995	
996	[[axj_nested_field]]
997	{axj_nested_field}::
998	A
999	{axl_motif}
1000	representing fields within object-typed fields enabling hierarchy.
1001	Maintains type safety through nested validation.
1002	
1003	[[axj_root_structure]]
1004	{axj_root_structure}::
1005	A
1006	{axl_motif}
1007	representing top-level JSON object schema.
1008	Entry point for JSON document validation.
1009	
1010	[[axj_field_cardinality]]
1011	{axj_field_cardinality}::
1012	A
1013	{axl_motif}
1014	representing required, optional, or repeated nature of a field.
1015	Maps to validation rules in implementation.
1016	
1017	[[axj_type_reference]]
1018	{axj_type_reference}::
1019	A
1020	{axl_motif}
1021	representing pointer to another
1022	{axj_structure}
1023	for composition.
1024	Maintains referential integrity across definitions.
1025	
1026	== Axial Argument Terms
1027	
1028	[[axa_argument]]
1029	{axa_argument}::
1030	A
1031	{axl_motif}
1032	representing command parameter accepting a value.
1033	Foundation for CLI and function interfaces.
1034	
1035	[[axa_positional]]
1036	{axa_positional}::
1037	A
1038	{axl_motif}
1039	representing order-determines-meaning parameter in command invocation.
1040	Position in argument list defines semantic role.
1041	
1042	[[axa_keyword]]
1043	{axa_keyword}::
1044	A
1045	{axl_motif}
1046	representing name-determines-meaning parameter with explicit identifier.
1047	Order-independent with explicit naming.
1048	
1049	[[axa_long_option]]
1050	{axa_long_option}::
1051	A
1052	{axl_motif}
1053	representing keyword argument with `--` prefix following GNU conventions.
1054	Uses hyphenated multi-word names.
1055	
1056	[[axa_short_option]]
1057	{axa_short_option}::
1058	A
1059	{axl_motif}
1060	representing keyword argument with single `-` prefix for brevity.
1061	Often provides shorthand for
1062	{axa_long_option}.
1063	
1064	[[axa_option_name]]
1065	{axa_option_name}::
1066	A
1067	{axl_motif}
1068	representing the identifier portion of a keyword argument.
1069	Defines the semantic meaning of the option.
1070	
1071	[[axa_option_value]]
1072	{axa_option_value}::
1073	A
1074	{axl_motif}
1075	representing the data portion following an option identifier.
1076	Subject to
1077	{axa_argument_type}
1078	constraints.
1079	
1080	[[axa_argument_type]]
1081	{axa_argument_type}::
1082	A
1083	{axl_motif}
1084	representing expected data format for an
1085	{axa_argument}.
1086	Enforces type safety at interface boundary.
1087	
1088	[[axa_required]]
1089	{axa_required}::
1090	A
1091	{axl_motif}
1092	representing attribute indicating an
1093	{axa_argument}
1094	must be provided.
1095	Absence triggers
1096	{axc_fatal}
1097	or error response.
1098	
1099	[[axa_optional]]
1100	{axa_optional}::
1101	A
1102	{axl_motif}
1103	representing attribute indicating an
1104	{axa_argument}
1105	may be omitted.
1106	Provides flexibility in interface usage.
1107	
1108	[[axa_defaulted]]
1109	{axa_defaulted}::
1110	A
1111	{axl_motif}
1112	representing attribute indicating an
1113	{axa_argument}
1114	has a fallback value.
1115	Specifies behavior when
1116	{axa_optional}
1117	argument omitted.
1118	
1119	[[axa_argument_list]]
1120	{axa_argument_list}::
1121	A
1122	{axl_motif}
1123	representing a structured section documenting an operation's arguments.
1124	{axl_voices}
1125	{axhopt_typed_parameter}
1126	/{axhopa_arg_parameter}
1127	in CLI context.
1128	Within an
1129	{axa_argument_list},
1130	{axd_dimension}
1131	annotations may precede bullet items to qualify argument usage without requiring anchors.
1132	Each item references a globally-defined argument term.
1133	The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
1134	+
1135	Example section header definition:
1136	+
1137	[source,asciidoc]
1138	----
1139	[[jjds_arguments]]
1140	//axl_voices axa_argument_list
1141	{jjds_arguments}::
1142	Section header introducing operation arguments.
1143	----
1144	+
1145	Example usage in an operation:
1146	+
1147	[source,asciidoc]
1148	----
1149	{jjds_arguments}
1150	
1151	//axd_required
1152	* {jjda_silks}
1153	
1154	//axd_optional axd_defaulted
1155	* {jjda_file}
1156	----
1157	
1158	[[axa_cli_option]]
1159	{axa_cli_option}::
1160	A
1161	{axl_motif}
1162	representing a command-line option definition.
1163	{axl_voicing_s}
1164	require the definition text to specify:
1165	+
1166	* The long form (e.g., `--file`)
1167	* The short form, or its absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
1168	+
1169	Example:
1170	+
1171	[source,asciidoc]
1172	----
1173	[[jjda_file]]
1174	//axl_voices axa_cli_option
1175	{jjda_file}::
1176	Path to the Gallops JSON file.
1177	Long: `--file`.
1178	Short: none.
1179	Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
1180	----
1181	
1182	[[axa_exit_uniform]]
1183	{axa_exit_uniform}::
1184	A
1185	{axl_motif}
1186	representing an Exit Status section that follows standard Unix conventions.
1187	{axl_voices}
1188	{axhoc_completion}
1189	in CLI context.
1190	Exit 0 indicates success; any non-zero exit indicates error.
1191	{axl_voicing_s}
1192	may use terse notation since the pattern is conventional.
1193	The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
1194	Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
1195	+
1196	Example section header definition:
1197	+
1198	[source,asciidoc]
1199	----
1200	[[jjds_exit_uniform]]
1201	//axl_voices axa_exit_uniform
1202	{jjds_exit_uniform}::
1203	Section header for operations with standard exit semantics.
1204	----
1205	+
1206	Example usage:
1207	+
1208	[source,asciidoc]
1209	----
1210	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
1211	----
1212	
1213	[[axa_exit_enumerated]]
1214	{axa_exit_enumerated}::
1215	A
1216	{axl_motif}
1217	representing an Exit Status section with distinct exit codes carrying specific meanings.
1218	{axl_voices}
1219	{axhoc_completion}
1220	in CLI context.
1221	{axl_voicing_s}
1222	require the definition text to list each exit code and its meaning.
1223	The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
1224	Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
1225	+
1226	Example section header definition:
1227	+
1228	[source,asciidoc]
1229	----
1230	[[jjds_exit_enumerated]]
1231	//axl_voices axa_exit_enumerated
1232	{jjds_exit_enumerated}::
1233	Section header for operations with distinct exit codes.
1234	----
1235	+
1236	Example usage:
1237	+
1238	[source,asciidoc]
1239	----
1240	{jjds_exit_enumerated}
1241	
1242	* 0 — found and valid
1243	* 1 — not found
1244	* 2 — found but malformed
1245	----
1246	
1247	== Axial Interface Terms
1248	
1249	[[axi_command_interface]]
1250	{axi_command_interface}::
1251	A
1252	{axl_motif}
1253	representing complete specification of a command's invocation pattern.
1254	Defines syntax and semantics for command usage.
1255	
1256	[[axi_subcommand]]
1257	{axi_subcommand}::
1258	A
1259	{axl_motif}
1260	representing nested command within parent command structure.
1261	Inherits context from parent command.
1262	
1263	[[axi_command_signature]]
1264	{axi_command_signature}::
1265	A
1266	{axl_motif}
1267	representing complete parameter specification for a command.
1268	Serves as contract for command invocation.
1269	
1270	[[axi_endpoint]]
1271	{axi_endpoint}::
1272	A
1273	{axl_motif}
1274	representing URL path accepting HTTP requests.
1275	Defines resource location and accepted operations.
1276	
1277	[[axi_api_method]]
1278	{axi_api_method}::
1279	A
1280	{axl_motif}
1281	representing HTTP verb combined with
1282	{axi_endpoint}
1283	specification.
1284	Includes request and response specifications.
1285	
1286	[[axi_url_parameter]]
1287	{axi_url_parameter}::
1288	A
1289	{axl_motif}
1290	representing query string key-value pair in URL.
1291	Appears after `?` in URL structure.
1292	
1293	[[axi_hash_parameter]]
1294	{axi_hash_parameter}::
1295	A
1296	{axl_motif}
1297	representing fragment identifier for client-side state.
1298	Persists state without server round-trips.
1299	
1300	[[axi_request_body]]
1301	{axi_request_body}::
1302	A
1303	{axl_motif}
1304	representing structured input payload for HTTP requests.
1305	Validated against
1306	{axj_structure}
1307	definitions.
1308	
1309	[[axi_response_body]]
1310	{axi_response_body}::
1311	A
1312	{axl_motif}
1313	representing structured output payload from HTTP responses.
1314	Conforms to documented
1315	{axj_structure}.
1316	
1317	[[axi_cli_interface]]
1318	{axi_cli_interface}::
1319	A
1320	{axl_motif}
1321	representing command-line invocation pattern specification.
1322	Documents
1323	{axa_argument_s}
1324	and behavior.
1325	
1326	[[axi_cli_program]]
1327	{axi_cli_program}::
1328	A
1329	{axl_motif}
1330	representing a Unix-style command-line program.
1331	Invoked from a shell with arguments.
1332	Writes results to stdout, errors to stderr.
1333	Returns exit status: 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
1334	Suitable for scripted, non-interactive use.
1335	
1336	[[axi_cli_command_group]]
1337	{axi_cli_command_group}::
1338	A
1339	{axl_motif}
1340	representing a namespace within a CLI that contains subcommands.
1341	Does not perform operations itself; serves as organizational container.
1342	May be nested within an
1343	{axi_cli_program}
1344	or another
1345	{axi_cli_command_group}.
1346	Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent.
1347	
1348	[[axi_cli_subcommand]]
1349	{axi_cli_subcommand}::
1350	A
1351	{axl_motif}
1352	representing a leaf operation within an
1353	{axi_cli_program}
1354	or
1355	{axi_cli_command_group}.
1356	Performs an actual operation; not a container for other commands.
1357	Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent: arguments, stdout, stderr, exit status.
1358	
1359	[[axi_rest_api]]
1360	{axi_rest_api}::
1361	A
1362	{axl_motif}
1363	representing resource-based web service interface.
1364	Maps operations to HTTP methods and resources.
1365	
1366	[[axi_rpc_interface]]
1367	{axi_rpc_interface}::
1368	A
1369	{axl_motif}
1370	representing remote procedure call pattern for service invocation.
1371	Emphasizes operation semantics over HTTP methods.
1372	
1373	[[axi_sse_endpoint]]
1374	{axi_sse_endpoint}::
1375	A
1376	{axl_motif}
1377	representing server-sent event stream for push notifications.
1378	Enables real-time updates to connected clients.
1379	
1380	[[axi_cc_slash_command]]
1381	{axi_cc_slash_command}::
1382	A
1383	{axl_motif}
1384	representing a Claude Code slash command skill artifact.
1385	Implemented as a `.claude/commands/*.md` file containing orchestration logic that the LLM executes as a skill.
1386	Slash commands coordinate multiple
1387	{axi_cli_subcommand}
1388	invocations, gather context, and apply workflow policies.
1389	
1390	[[axi_cc_claudemd_verb]]
1391	{axi_cc_claudemd_verb}::
1392	A
1393	{axl_motif}
1394	representing an action verb defined in a
1395	{axi_cc_claudemd_section}
1396	that maps user intent to tool-layer operations.
1397	The verb exists as a concept in the LLM context — either mapping directly to a single
1398	{axi_cli_subcommand}
1399	(direct verb) or orchestrating multiple operations (multistep verb implemented by a
1400	{axi_cc_slash_command}).
1401	
1402	[[axi_cc_claudemd_section]]
1403	{axi_cc_claudemd_section}::
1404	A
1405	{axl_motif}
1406	representing a managed section within a project CLAUDE.md file.
1407	Provides persistent LLM context that bridges specification-layer concepts to runtime execution.
1408	Typically contains command references, vocabulary mappings, and operational guidance.
1409	
1410	[[axi_protocol]]
1411	{axi_protocol}::
1412	A
1413	{axl_motif}
1414	representing the bilateral discipline binding two
1415	{axo_actor_s}
1416	— a caller and a callee — through a defined sequence of call and return envelopes.
1417	Distinct from
1418	{axi_rpc_interface}
1419	which describes one side's view of a service;
1420	{axi_protocol}
1421	is the relationship itself, owning transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions.
1422	A protocol quoin carries transport via
1423	{axd_dimension_s}
1424	(e.g.,
1425	{axd_file_bus}),
1426	lifecycle presumptions (e.g.,
1427	{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}),
1428	and concurrency discipline as dimensions on the primary voicing.
1429	Structural detail — the roster of transactions, call/return envelopes, and member types — lives at the
1430	{axl_detail_site}
1431	under
1432	{axhpb_protocol}
1433	and nested hierarchy markers.
1434	
1435	== Axial Dimension Terms
1436	
1437	{axd_dimension_s}
1438	are modifiers that appear in
1439	{axl_annotation_s}
1440	to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
1441	Unlike primary
1442	{axl_motif_s},
1443	{axd_dimension_s}
1444	do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
1445	Applicability of
1446	{axd_dimension_s}
1447	depends on serialization format context.
1448	
1449	[[axd_dimension]]
1450	{axd_dimension}::
1451	A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
1452	{axl_annotation}.
1453	{axd_dimension_s}
1454	appear after the primary
1455	{axl_motif}
1456	in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`
1457	
1458	=== Value Dimensions
1459	
1460	These
1461	{axd_dimension_s}
1462	describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
1463	They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).
1464	
1465	[[axd_required]]
1466	{axd_required}::
1467	Member or argument must be present.
1468	Absence triggers validation error or
1469	{axc_fatal}.
1470	
1471	[[axd_optional]]
1472	{axd_optional}::
1473	Member or argument may be omitted.
1474	Absence is valid and handled gracefully.
1475	
1476	[[axd_nullable]]
1477	{axd_nullable}::
1478	Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
1479	Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
1480	Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.
1481	
1482	[[axd_none]]
1483	{axd_none}::
1484	Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
1485	Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
1486	Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.
1487	
1488	[[axd_defaulted]]
1489	{axd_defaulted}::
1490	Has fallback value when absent.
1491	Voicing should specify the default value in prose.
1492	
1493	[[axd_repeated]]
1494	{axd_repeated}::
1495	May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
1496	Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
1497	Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.
1498	
1499	[[axd_list]]
1500	{axd_list}::
1501	Single value containing delimited multiple values.
1502	Distinct from
1503	{axd_repeated}
1504	which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
1505	Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
1506	Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).
1507	
1508	=== Format-Specific Dimensions
1509	
1510	These
1511	{axd_dimension_s}
1512	apply only to specific serialization formats.
1513	Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.
1514	
1515	[[axd_field_number]]
1516	{axd_field_number}::
1517	Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
1518	Required for protobuf format.
1519	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
1520	
1521	[[axd_wire_type]]
1522	{axd_wire_type}::
1523	Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
1524	Required for protobuf format.
1525	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
1526	
1527	[[axd_endianness]]
1528	{axd_endianness}::
1529	Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
1530	Required for binary format.
1531	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.
1532	
1533	[[axd_xml_form]]
1534	{axd_xml_form}::
1535	Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
1536	Required for XML format.
1537	Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.
1538	
1539	[[axd_property]]
1540	{axd_property}::
1541	JSON
1542	{axr_member}
1543	encoded as a named property within its containing
1544	{axr_record_json}.
1545	The wire-level key is a fixed sprue declared at the definition site;
1546	the 1-arity sprue lookahead applies and is fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
1547	{mcm_sprue}
1548	in the following text.
1549	Required for JSON
1550	{axr_member_s}
1551	whose value sits at a named slot.
1552	Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.
1553	
1554	[[axd_positional]]
1555	{axd_positional}::
1556	JSON
1557	{axr_member}
1558	encoded by structural position rather than by a named property.
1559	The member's value provides the parent map's key directly;
1560	no separate sprue exists in the wire form.
1561	Suppresses the 1-arity sprue lookahead — under
1562	{axd_positional},
1563	{axr_member}
1564	becomes 0-arity for sprue.
1565	Required for JSON
1566	{axr_member_s}
1567	whose value is the parent map's key (e.g., a record's identity field encoded as the map entry's key).
1568	Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.
1569	
1570	Exactly one of
1571	{axd_property}
1572	or
1573	{axd_positional}
1574	must be present on every
1575	{axr_member}
1576	under an
1577	{axr_record_json}
1578	carrier whose value participates in the JSON wire form.
1579	Members that are computed, ephemeral, or stored outside the JSON wire are not
1580	{axr_member_s}
1581	of the
1582	{axr_record_json}
1583	and should not carry the
1584	{axr_member}
1585	voicing in the first place.
1586	
1587	[[axd_string]]
1588	{axd_string}::
1589	{axt_enum_value}
1590	encoded as a literal string token in the wire form.
1591	Carries a 1-arity sprue lookahead, fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
1592	{mcm_sprue}
1593	in the following text — the literal wire-level value as it appears in the carrier.
1594	The
1595	{mcm_sprue}
1596	is the exact wire-name; the
1597	{mcm_quoin}
1598	is the conceptual enum-value identity.
1599	Carrier-agnostic: applies wherever an
1600	{axt_enumeration}
1601	serializes its values as string tokens (JSON strings, bash regime values, YAML scalars, env-var values).
1602	Forbidden when the wire form is non-string (numeric, bitmask, object-tagged, positional, untagged variant) — those encodings will mint companion dimensions.
1603	
1604	=== Lifecycle Dimensions
1605	
1606	These
1607	{axd_dimension_s}
1608	qualify
1609	{axo_procedure}
1610	voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
1611	Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
1612	{axo_procedure}
1613	voicing.
1614	Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
1615	
1616	[[axd_transient]]
1617	{axd_transient}::
1618	{axo_procedure}
1619	executes and completes.
1620	The default lifecycle for most procedures.
1621	{axhoc_completion}
1622	documents exit status or return value.
1623	
1624	[[axd_longrunning]]
1625	{axd_longrunning}::
1626	{axo_procedure}
1627	runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
1628	{axhoc_completion}
1629	documents startup success criteria, not termination.
1630	Requires
1631	{axhos_step} (startup)
1632	and
1633	{axhos_step} (shutdown)
1634	sections.
1635	May include
1636	{axhos_step} (health, signals, recovery)
1637	sections.
1638	
1639	[[axd_periodic]]
1640	{axd_periodic}::
1641	{axo_procedure}
1642	executes on a schedule.
1643	{axhoc_completion}
1644	applies per-run.
1645	Requires
1646	{axhos_step} (schedule)
1647	section.
1648	May include
1649	{axhos_step} (idempotency, overlap)
1650	sections.
1651	
1652	=== Regime Dimensions
1653	
1654	These
1655	{axd_dimension_s}
1656	qualify regime structure annotations.
1657	
1658	[[axd_conditional]]
1659	{axd_conditional}::
1660	Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
1661	A group annotated with
1662	{axd_conditional}
1663	requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
1664	{axhrgc_gate}
1665	specifying the activation criteria.
1666	
1667	[[axrd_singleton]]
1668	{axrd_singleton}::
1669	Regime has exactly one assignment source.
1670	No enumeration or cross-instance operations apply.
1671	
1672	[[axrd_manifold]]
1673	{axrd_manifold}::
1674	Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
1675	Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.
1676	
1677	=== Protocol Dimensions
1678	
1679	These
1680	{axd_dimension_s}
1681	qualify
1682	{axi_protocol}
1683	voicings to specify transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions of the bilateral discipline.
1684	
1685	[[axd_file_bus]]
1686	{axd_file_bus}::
1687	Transport dimension on an
1688	{axi_protocol}:
1689	participants communicate via a shared filesystem directory acting as the wire.
1690	The caller writes call envelopes as files; the callee observes atomic file appearance via rename(2) (write-temp-then-rename) and writes return envelopes the same way.
1691	No sockets, no in-process shared memory — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire.
1692	+
1693	*Index discipline:* participants coordinate transaction ordering via monotonic indices assigned by the caller and embedded in filenames or envelope bodies.
1694	Indices are session-local and reset on callee restart; the caller detects restart via index-collision or an explicit readiness exchange.
1695	Filename conventions for call and return envelopes are declared in the voicing spec, not in this motif.
1696	+
1697	*Atomicity:* every envelope write uses the rename(2) idiom so receivers never observe partial content.
1698	+
1699	*Wire-format neutrality:*
1700	{axd_file_bus}
1701	is transport-only; envelope contents are described by separate
1702	{axr_record_s}
1703	voicings (e.g.,
1704	{axr_record_json}).
1705	
1706	[[axd_disjoint_lifecycle]]
1707	{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}::
1708	Lifecycle dimension on an
1709	{axi_protocol}:
1710	the caller and callee have independent start and stop lifecycles.
1711	Neither actor can assume the other exists when it itself begins;
1712	each call must handle the possibility that the peer is absent, starting, or no longer the same instance that handled the previous call.
1713	+
1714	Implies the caller must observe callee presence before committing to a transaction, and must gracefully degrade or retry when the callee is unavailable.
1715	The specific observation mechanism (readiness probe, file-presence check, timeout) is declared in the protocol's detail site, not in this motif.
1716	+
1717	Contrasts with shared-session protocols where caller and callee share a bounded lifetime (e.g., an HTTP request-response pair scoped to a single process).
1718	
1719	=== Dimension Applicability
1720	
1721	Per-format guidance for
1722	{axd_dimension}
1723	usage:
1724	
1725	[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
1726	|===
1727	|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML
1728	
1729	|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
1730	|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
1731	|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
1732	|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
1733	|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
1734	|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
1735	|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
1736	|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
1737	|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
1738	|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
1739	|===
1740

= Axial Lexicon Specification
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// axc_: Axial Control (orchestration control terms) → mcm_form_deflist
// axe_: Axial Environment (execution context patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
// axo_: Axial Operation (operation patterns) → mcm_form_deflist, mcm_form_section for sequences
// axt_: Axial Type (type system definitions) → mcm_form_deflist
// axr_: Axial Record (record/member structure patterns) → mcm_form_section for records, mcm_form_deflist for members
// axj_: Axial JSON (JSON-specific concerns) → mcm_form_section for structures, mcm_form_bullet for fields
// axa_: Axial Argument (command argument patterns) → mcm_form_deflist, mcm_form_bullet under parent
// axi_: Axial Interface (interface patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
// axk_: Axial Key Premise (design constraints) → mcm_form_deflist
// axl_: Axial Lexicon (motif vocabulary terms) → mcm_form_deflist
// axs_: Axial Section (documentation section motifs) → mcm_form_deflist
// axd_: Axial Dimension (member/argument characteristic modifiers) → mcm_form_deflist
// axf_: Axial Format (serialization/carriage formats) → mcm_form_deflist
// axrg_: Axial Regime (configuration system patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
// axvr_: Axial Voicing Regime (definition-site regime annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
// axvo_: Axial Voicing Operation (definition-site operation annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
// axhr*_: Axial Hierarchy Regime (standalone hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
// axho*_: Axial Hierarchy Operation (standalone operation hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
// axhp*_: Axial Hierarchy Protocol (standalone protocol hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
// axpo*_: Axial Product-of-Operation (definition-site voicings for operation outputs) → mcm_form_deflist
// axve_: Axial Voicing Entity (definition-site entity annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
// axhe*_: Axial Hierarchy Entity (standalone entity hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
// xref_: External specification references
//
// Extended Type Categories (domain-specific type subcategories):
// axtu_: Axial Type Universal (generic infrastructure types) → mcm_form_deflist
// axtg_: Axial Type Google (Google Cloud Platform types) → mcm_form_deflist
// axtw_: Axial Type AWS (Amazon Web Services types) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// axta_: Axial Type Azure (Microsoft Azure types) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// axth_: Axial Type GitHub (GitHub types) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// Reserved: axtc_ (Cloudflare), axti_ (IBM), axtm_ (avoid - Microsoft confusion)
//
// Extended Infrastructure Categories (domain-specific infrastructure patterns):
// axig_: Axial Infrastructure Google (GCP infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist
// axiw_: Axial Infrastructure AWS (AWS infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// axia_: Axial Infrastructure Azure (Azure infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
// axih_: Axial Infrastructure GitHub (GitHub infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//
